audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Transjordan settlement negotiation text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Reuben/Gad livestock request, Moses' fear of communal discouragement, Kadesh memory, armed-vanguard pledge, legal conditions, family settlements, east-Jordan allocation, half-Manasseh expansion, and place renaming.

verses.restored: Sons of Reuben and sons of Gad had very great abundance of livestock. They saw land of Jazer and land of Gilead, and look, the place was a place for livestock.
Sons of Gad and sons of Reuben came and spoke to Moses and Eleazar the priest and leaders of congregation, saying,
Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon—
the land that YHWH struck before congregation of Israel—is a land for livestock, and your servants have livestock.
They said, If we have found favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as possession; do not make us cross Jordan.
Moses said to sons of Gad and sons of Reuben, Shall your brothers go to war while you sit here?
Why do you turn aside heart of children of Israel from crossing into land that YHWH has given them?
Thus your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see land.
They went up to wadi of Eshcol and saw land, and turned aside heart of children of Israel so they would not enter land YHWH had given them.
Anger of YHWH burned on that day, and he swore, saying,
Surely none of men who came up from Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see ground I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, for they did not follow fully after me,
except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun, for they followed fully after YHWH.
Anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he made them wander in wilderness forty years, until all generation doing evil in eyes of YHWH came to an end.
And look, you have risen in place of your fathers, a brood of sinful men, to add still more to burning anger of YHWH against Israel.
If you turn back from after him, he will again leave them in wilderness, and you will destroy all this people.
They came near to him and said, Sheepfolds we will build here for our livestock and cities for our little ones.
But we ourselves will equip ourselves, hastening before children of Israel until we bring them to their place; our little ones shall dwell in fortified cities because of inhabitants of land.
We will not return to our houses until each one of children of Israel has inherited his inheritance.
For we will not inherit with them across Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us on east side of Jordan.
Moses said to them, If you do this thing, if you equip yourselves before YHWH for war,
and every equipped one among you crosses Jordan before YHWH until he has driven out his enemies from before him,
and land is subdued before YHWH, afterward you shall return and be free from obligation before YHWH and Israel; and this land shall be your possession before YHWH.
But if you do not do so, look, you have sinned/misaligned against YHWH; know your sin/misalignment will find you.
Build for yourselves cities for your little ones and folds for your flocks, and what has gone out from your mouth, do.
Sons of Gad and sons of Reuben said to Moses, saying, Your servants will do as my lord commands.
Our little ones, our wives, our livestock, and all our beasts shall be there in cities of Gilead,
but your servants will cross over, everyone equipped for host, before YHWH to war, as my lord speaks.
Moses commanded concerning them Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun and heads of fathers' houses of tribes of children of Israel.

Moses said to them, If sons of Gad and sons of Reuben cross Jordan with you, everyone equipped for war before YHWH, and land is subdued before you, then you shall give them land of Gilead as possession.
But if they do not cross equipped with you, then they shall receive possessions in your midst in land of Canaan.
Sons of Gad and sons of Reuben answered, saying, What YHWH has spoken to your servants, so we will do.
We will cross equipped before YHWH into land of Canaan, but possession of our inheritance shall be with us across Jordan.

Moses gave to sons of Gad and sons of Reuben and half tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph kingdom of Sihon king of Amorite and kingdom of Og king of Bashan, land with its cities in borders, cities of land around.
Sons of Gad built Dibon, Ataroth, and Aroer,
Atroth-shophan, Jazer, and Jogbehah,
Beth-nimrah and Beth-haran, fortified cities, and folds for flocks.
Sons of Reuben built Heshbon, Elealeh, and Kiriathaim,
Nebo and Baal-meon—their names being changed—and Sibmah; they called by names the cities they built.
Sons of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead and captured it and dispossessed Amorite who was in it.
Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he dwelt in it.
Jair son of Manasseh went and captured their villages and called them Havvoth-jair/Villages of Jair.
Nobah went and captured Kenath and its villages and called it Nobah by his name.

translation_notes: The request begins from livestock abundance and ecological suitability, not simple refusal of covenant responsibility.
Moses initially hears the request through the trauma of Numbers 13-14 and fears another collapse of communal courage.
The tribes negotiate a conditional settlement: they will assist the larger community before returning to their east-Jordan homes.
Women and children remain in fortified settlements while men fight; their vulnerability and lack of direct voice should remain visible.
The final verses describe conquest, dispossession, rebuilding, and renaming. These are ancient territorial claims and must not authorize modern colonization or erasure of prior inhabitants.
The phrase 'your sin will find you' refers in context to breaking a public communal commitment, not a universal tool for threatening people.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 32 to glorify militarized masculinity, abandon family caregivers, threaten people with hidden punishment, or sanctify modern settlement and dispossession. Its constructive thread is accountable negotiation: resources, obligations, risks, and promises are made explicit before land is allocated.
